service-os.label: Service OS
service-os.summary: A calm, dashboard-flavored direction that treats the laundry storefront like a well-run operations console: clear status, honest pricing, and obvious next steps. Bricolage Grotesque display over a Public Sans body, with a Space Mono accent for figures and labels.

heavy.label: Brand-Heavy
heavy.direction: Leads with the WaveMAX teal as the primary surface accent — saturated brand fields, brand-colored hero, and the WaveMAX wordmark treated as a first-class element.
heavy.brandTreatment: WaveMAX teal (#0c93ad) is the lead color and carries large surfaces and CTAs; the deep navy anchors structure. The franchise reads unmistakably as 'a WaveMAX'.
heavy.noticeLocation: The §12.2 independent-operator notice ("independently owned and operated by CRHS Enterprises, LLC under a franchise license from WaveMAX Franchise, LLC") sits in the footer, set quietly to match the chrome.
heavy.differsFromCorporate: Same brand color, but a cleaner, more operational layout than the corporate marketing site — status-driven sections and a pricing-forward hero instead of stock-photo banners.
heavy.conversionNote: High brand trust up front; the saturated CTAs draw the eye to 'schedule pickup'. Watch contrast on teal-on-teal CTA states for accessibility.
heavy.performanceNote: Single inline <style> block, no scripts in the render, one deferred Google Maps iframe on contact pages. Fast first paint; map is the only third-party weight.

light.label: Brand-Light
light.direction: Pulls the saturated brand back to a restrained accent. Mostly paper/ink neutrals with teal used as a precise highlight (the emerald accent becomes the lead instead of full brand teal).
light.brandTreatment: Brand teal recedes to trim, links, and small accents; the mint/emerald accent leads. Reads as a premium, independent local operator that happens to be WaveMAX-affiliated.
light.noticeLocation: Same §12.2 footer notice; in the lighter palette it sits even more discreetly within the neutral chrome.
light.differsFromCorporate: Much more neutral and editorial than corporate — generous whitespace, quieter color, lets typography and copy carry the page rather than brand color blocks.
light.conversionNote: Lower visual pressure; relies on clear hierarchy and copy to convert. Better for an audience that distrusts hard-sell color; the single accent CTA must stay prominent enough.
light.performanceNote: Identical render budget to heavy — one inline <style>, no scripts, map iframe only on contact. The lighter palette has no extra cost.
